market_screens: Kalshi spread market: CLE +4.5 / DET -4.5, user locked No on Detroit wins by over 4.5 points
Kalshi rebound market: Tobias Harris 8+ rebounds
Book screen: DET team total mostly 107.5 to 108.5, CLE team total mostly 104.5 to 105.5
Book screen: full game total mostly 212.5 to 213.5, Detroit favored around -3.5 to -4.5

DET_injury_watch: Kevin Huerter questionable - left adductor strain / possible eighth straight absence

recent_tracking_window.description: Player tracking rebound chances from prior CLE-DET game used to recalibrate Game 5 rebound market.

locked_positions.selection_display: No - Detroit wins by over 4.5 points

model_read.edge_summary: Cleveland +4.5 is supported by rolling-window Game 4 inputs: Cleveland second-half correction profile, Mitchell takeover tail, free throw creation, and Detroit turnover-pressure fade risk.

risk_flags: Detroit at home with market favorite status
Cleveland margin still exposed to late foul-game variance
Detroit injury uncertainty can either hurt spacing or concentrate usage into Cade/Tobias/Duren

model_read.edge_summary: Tobias 8+ is a plus-money micro ladder. It is not a high-certainty straight projection, but the price is above estimated fair value based on rolling-window chance quality.

tracking_evidence.read: Tobias showed active rebound involvement, not just passive long-board collection. His 88.9% adjusted chance rate and 5 contested boards made him the top Detroit rebound ladder signal over Duren.

risk_flags: Threshold requires 8 exactly or better; median remains 7
If Robinson/LeVert/Huerter statuses shift Detroit rotation size/spacing, rebound distribution may change
Duren or Paul Reed can siphon interior boards if Detroit rebalances frontcourt minutes

game4_carryover_signals: Cleveland can outperform raw halftime score when creation and free throw access remain strong.
Detroit turnover pressure is a live downgrade signal, especially if Cade carries heavy initiation load.
Cleveland spread/margin markets carry better value than Detroit favorite pricing when the market overweights Detroit record/home status.
Rebound tracking downgraded Jalen Duren despite high chances because conversion was extremely poor, shifting Detroit rebound ladder attention toward Tobias Harris.
injury_context: Duncan Robinson questionable with back issue may reduce Detroit spacing or push more usage/minutes to Ausar, Jenkins, or Harris.
Huerter questionable and possibly still out keeps Detroit wing shooting depth thin.
LeVert questionable with heel issue matters because he supplied 24 points in Game 4 and can change bench creation/rebound distribution.

liveflow_watchlist.trigger: Tobias Harris has 4+ rebounds by halftime or 6+ rebound chances by halftime
liveflow_watchlist.action: Keep 8+ position live; consider no cash-out unless price becomes strongly profitable.

liveflow_watchlist.trigger: Tobias Harris has 2 or fewer rebound chances by halftime
liveflow_watchlist.action: Downgrade position; evaluate cash-out only if market still offers acceptable recovery.

liveflow_watchlist.trigger: Cleveland trails by 6 or fewer entering halftime with Mitchell/Harden/Mobley minutes clean
Detroit leads by 10+ with low turnover count and strong Cade half-court control
liveflow_watchlist.action: Spread position risk rises; watch for cash-out/liquidity if value deteriorates sharply.
